failingTest: None yet — this is a feature addition; new tests must be written to validate the unsupported-units banner and exclusion logic.
fileMap: internal/report/card.go — modify — partition units by language support, add banner to text/markdown output, exclude unsupported from aggregates
internal/report/badge.go — modify — exclude unsupported units from badge grade/score calculations
internal/report/health.go — modify — exclude unsupported units from health aggregate (if applicable)
internal/report/detailed.go — modify — reflect unsupported status in detailed report output
internal/report/full.go — modify — reflect unsupported status in full report output
internal/report/report_tree.go — modify — reflect unsupported status in report tree
internal/report/card_test.go — create or modify — add test cases for unsupported-language units in card generation
internal/report/badge_test.go — create or modify — add test cases for unsupported-language exclusion in badges
testdata/ — create — add fixture file(s) in an unsupported language (e.g. .swift) for integration-style tests
fixApproach: At report-generation time in card.go, iterate over all unit CertificationRecords and partition them by calling analysis.ForLanguage(unit.Language). Units where this returns nil are unsupported — they receive a derived Grade of "N/A" (or similar placeholder), are excluded from GPA/distribution/average aggregates, and are listed in a new banner section. The banner is rendered in both FormatCardText and FormatCardMarkdown. Badge generation in badge.go reads the same supported-only aggregates. Health/detailed/full reports inherit the partitioning. New testdata with an unsupported-language fixture (e.g. a .swift file) validates the banner appears, aggregates exclude it, and supported-language tests remain unchanged.
openQuestions: Where exactly is the unsupported-status derivation best placed — in card.go before GenerateCard is called, or inside GenerateCard itself? (Investigation suggests inside GenerateCard, checking analysis.ForLanguage per unit.)
Should the unsupported banner appear only in the Markdown card (FormatCardMarkdown), only in the text card (FormatCardText), or both? (Likely both, matching existing format patterns.)
steps: 1. Read internal/report/card.go, badge.go, health.go to understand current CertificationRecord structure and how grades flow into aggregates (GPA, distribution, averages).
2. Read internal/analysis registry (or wherever analysis.ForLanguage is defined) to confirm the nil-means-unsupported contract.
3. In card.go, add a helper that partitions []CertificationRecord into supported / unsupported slices using analysis.ForLanguage.
4. Modify GenerateCard (or its callers) to use only supported units for GPA, grade distribution counts, and all averages.
5. Add the unsupported-units banner to FormatCardText: '⚠️ N units in unsupported languages (lang1, lang2, ...) — not analyzed. Scores for these units are withheld.'
6. Add the equivalent banner to FormatCardMarkdown.
7. Add denominator clarification to the aggregate section in both text and markdown formats: 'Based on X analyzable units; Y units skipped.'
8. In badge.go, ensure badge grade/score reads from supported-only aggregates.
9. In health.go, detailed.go, full.go, report_tree.go, ensure unsupported units are surfaced with N/A grades and do not distort any aggregate.

10. Write new test cases: (a) mixed supported+unsupported units produce the banner and correct aggregates, (b) all-supported case produces no banner, (c) all-unsupported case produces the banner with zero supported units.
11. Run go test ./... -count=1 and fix any failures.
12. Run go build -o build/bin/certify ./cmd/certify/ to confirm clean build.
summary: Add an unsupported-units banner to the report card that counts/lists unsupported languages and excludes them from all aggregate grade calculations.
uiStateNotes: Report card gains a new banner section: '⚠️ N units in unsupported languages (Swift, Shell, ...) — not analyzed. Scores for these units are withheld.'
Aggregate section gains denominator clarification: 'Based on X analyzable units; Y units skipped.'
